title: Root CLI Responsibility Split
goal: Implement #488 by documenting root cli.py responsibility buckets and extracting one low-risk non-core cluster without changing public CLI behavior.
non_goals: Do not rewrite the CLI from scratch.
Do not move lifecycle mutation or live repo inspection into declarative contracts.
Do not start generated Python/TypeScript CLI package work in this slice.

intent.outcome: cli.py becomes easier to reason about because responsibility buckets are explicit and at least one renderer/helper cluster no longer lives in the main procedural file.
intent.constraints: Stay on current branch; preserve command behavior; keep mutation and live inspection runtime-owned.
intent.latitude: Choose the smallest useful helper extraction and contract map update.
intent.escalation: Stop if the extraction would require broad parser or lifecycle rewrites.

execution.next_step: Archive this plan, commit, close #488, refresh external evidence, then continue #485 with the next routed child.
execution.proof: Run focused report/defaults tests, full workspace CLI tests, ruff, contract checks, proof selector, and planning closeout.

invariants: Lifecycle mutation and live inspection stay procedural-owned.

intent_continuity.larger intended outcome: Keep the root orchestrator thin, boring, generic, and strictly bounded.

required_continuation.activation trigger: After #488 closes, reingest and promote the next module-boundary-compression child.

iterative_follow_through.what this slice enabled: Future module-profile and adapter-boundary work can rely on a clearer root CLI responsibility map.
iterative_follow_through.intentionally deferred: Deep parser generation, lifecycle primitive extraction, and generic module manifest implementation.
iterative_follow_through.proof achieved now: report-router rendering helpers moved out of cli.py and python_runtime_boundary records the split.
iterative_follow_through.validation still needed: Archive plan and commit after closeout checks.
iterative_follow_through.next likely slice: #490 or #510 depending on refreshed issue priority.

intent_interpretation.literal request: Split root cli.py responsibilities into runtime primitives, renderers, and orchestration glue.
intent_interpretation.inferred intended outcome: Make root CLI growth harder to hide in one procedural file while preserving a small, stable command surface.
intent_interpretation.chosen concrete what: Move report-router rendering helpers to reporting_support.py and record responsibility buckets in python_runtime_boundary.json.
intent_interpretation.review guidance: Review whether the extracted support module is still generic and whether the CLI wrappers are only glue.

execution_bounds.allowed paths: cli.py, reporting_support.py, python_runtime_boundary.json, focused tests, planning surfaces.
execution_bounds.required validation commands: focused report/defaults tests, full workspace CLI tests, ruff, contract tooling, proof selector, planning checks.
execution_bounds.ask-before-refactor threshold: Stop before broad parser, lifecycle, config-loader, or package topology changes.
execution_bounds.stop before touching: pyproject dependencies, generated packages, lifecycle mutation semantics, public command definitions.

stop_conditions.stop when: One non-core cluster is extracted, responsibility mapping is updated, and existing behavior is proven.
stop_conditions.escalate when boundary reached: The smallest useful extraction needs a larger architecture change.
stop_conditions.escalate on scope drift: The work becomes generated CLI package implementation or command-interface redesign.

context_budget.live working set: cli.py report-router helpers, reporting_support.py, python_runtime_boundary.json, tests/test_workspace_cli.py, issue #488.
context_budget.recoverable later: Full cli.py decomposition and generated CLI package roadmap.
context_budget.externalize before shift: Record any larger extraction target as a follow-up issue if discovered.
context_budget.pre-work memory pull: Not needed unless compact output points to Memory.
context_budget.tiny resumability note: Bounded helper extraction only; preserve CLI behavior.
context_budget.context-shift triggers: Parser generation, lifecycle behavior, or broad report contract redesign.

delegated_judgment.requested outcome: Make root CLI responsibilities explicit and extract a low-risk non-core cluster.
delegated_judgment.hard constraints: Preserve behavior and keep runtime-owned behavior in Python.
delegated_judgment.agent may decide locally: Exact support-module API and responsibility-map wording.

active_milestone.scope: Extract report-router helpers and update root CLI responsibility metadata.
active_milestone.ready: #488 is the selected next child for #485.

immediate_next_action: Move report-router helper logic out of cli.py while keeping thin wrappers or direct imports for compatibility.
validation_commands: uv run pytest tests/test_contract_tooling.py -q -k "python_runtime_boundary or generated_command_adapter_module"
uv run ruff check src/agentic_workspace/cli.py src/agentic_workspace/reporting_support.py tests/test_contract_tooling.py
uv run ruff check src tests

uv run agentic-workspace proof --target . --changed src/agentic_workspace/cli.py src/agentic_workspace/reporting_support.py src/agentic_workspace/contracts/python_runtime_boundary.json tests/test_contract_tooling.py .agentic-workspace/planning/state.toml .agentic-workspace/planning/execplans/root-cli-responsibility-split.plan.json --format json
uv run agentic-workspace defaults --section root_cli_authority --format json
uv run agentic-workspace doctor --target . --modules planning --format json
completion_criteria: cli.py has a documented responsibility map or points to one.
At least one clearly non-core helper cluster is extracted or marked for extraction.
CLI behavior is preserved by focused and full tests.
Future additions have a clearer owner than adding more code to cli.py.

execution_run.what happened: Moved report-router profile selection, section hints, ordinary-agent path, and compact router shaping from cli.py into reporting_support.py; left cli.py as thin command wiring; updated python_runtime_boundary and tests.
execution_run.scope touched: Root CLI helper extraction, runtime-boundary contract metadata, contract-tooling tests, planning closeout.
execution_run.validations run: focused report tests, focused runtime-boundary tests, contract tooling, combined workspace CLI/contract tests, ruff, proof selector, root CLI authority query, planning doctor, report smoke.
execution_run.result for continuation: #488 is satisfied; #490 and #510-#514 remain routed under #485.
execution_run.next step: Archive this plan, commit, close #488, refresh external evidence, then continue the module-boundary-compression lane.

finished_run_review.scope respected: yes; no parser, lifecycle mutation, package topology, or public command behavior changed.
finished_run_review.proof status: passed selected proof lanes and focused/full local validation.
finished_run_review.intent served: yes; cli.py now delegates report-router rendering and the responsibility map names the owner boundary.
finished_run_review.config compliance: used compact package summary/report/proof/doctor/defaults workflow and checked-in plan closeout.
finished_run_review.misinterpretation risk: low; this was a bounded extraction, not a claim that cli.py decomposition is complete.
finished_run_review.follow-on decision: archive this slice but keep #485 open through #490 and #510-#514.

proof_report.validation proof: Local checks pass for report behavior, runtime-boundary contract tooling, full workspace CLI tests, ruff, proof selection, and planning doctor.
proof_report.evidence for \"proof achieved\" state: report-router tests still pass; python_runtime_boundary declares report-router-rendering owner_modules as reporting_support plus cli; root_cli_authority output names the split.

intent_satisfaction.evidence of intent satisfaction: cli.py has a responsibility-map owner in python_runtime_boundary and one non-core renderer cluster is extracted into reporting_support.py with test coverage.

system_intent_alignment.relevant system intent: Root orchestrator should stay thin, generic, and bounded; implementation work should not keep expanding cli.py.
system_intent_alignment.slice shaping bias: Prefer one small extraction plus responsibility metadata over a large rewrite.
system_intent_alignment.broader-lane validation question: Does this make #490 generic module profiles easier to add without growing cli.py?

closure_check.why this decision is honest: The bounded slice is complete and remaining intent is routed to a checked-in continuation owner.
closure_check.evidence carried forward: Prepared closeout records intent satisfaction, closure decision, proof evidence, and distillation buckets.
closure_check.reopen trigger: Reopen when .agentic-workspace/planning/state.toml roadmap lane module-boundary-compression activates a fresh bounded slice.

execution_summary.outcome delivered: Report-router rendering extracted from cli.py and responsibility map updated.
execution_summary.validation confirmed: Focused and combined CLI/contract tests, ruff, contract tooling, proof selector, root authority query, planning checks, and report smoke passed.
execution_summary.post-work posterity capture: Archived execplan plus python_runtime_boundary contract metadata.
execution_summary.knowledge promoted (Memory/Docs/Config): Durable owner mapping lives in python_runtime_boundary.json; no Memory update needed.
execution_summary.resume from: Reingest open issues and choose the next #485 child after closing #488.

signals found: report --section repo_friction still flags cli.py as a large-file hotspot; this slice reduces that hotspot by extracting report-router helpers.
signals fixed: one report-router helper cluster no longer lives in cli.py

discard.summary: Command-by-command execution detail stays in the archived proof record.

memory.summary: Durable owner mapping lives in python_runtime_boundary.json; no Memory update needed.
